name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
tagline: Google's flagship reasoning model for the most complex agentic and coding tasks.
seoTitle: Gemini 3.1 Pro: Google's Flagship Reasoning Model
metaDescription: Gemini 3.1 Pro is Google's flagship model, with a 1M-token context window, multimodal input, and top scores on reasoning, agentic, and coding benchmarks.
overview: Gemini 3.1 Pro is Google DeepMind's flagship model, released February 19, 2026 as the latest member of the Gemini 3 family. It is positioned for tasks where a simple answer isn't enough, with advanced reasoning, improved agentic capabilities, and strong multimodal understanding across text, audio, images, video, and entire code repositories.

The model accepts up to a 1M-token input context window and produces up to 64K output tokens, making it suited to long-context understanding over large documents and codebases. Knowledge cutoff is January 2025. It is available in preview via the Gemini API on Google AI Studio, Gemini CLI, Vertex AI, Gemini Enterprise, and the Gemini app for Google AI Pro/Ultra users.

On Google's published evaluations, Gemini 3.1 Pro reaches 94.3% on GPQA Diamond, 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 (described as more than double the reasoning performance of Gemini 3 Pro), and 80.6% on SWE-Bench Verified, placing it among the strongest models on reasoning, agentic, and coding tasks.
strengths: Frontier-level reasoning, with 94.3% on GPQA Diamond and 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2
Strong agentic and coding performance (80.6% SWE-Bench Verified, 68.5% Terminal-Bench 2.0)
1M-token input context for long-document and whole-repository understanding
Native multimodal input across text, image, audio, video, and PDF
Strong long-context retrieval (84.9% on MRCR v2 at 128k)
useCases: Agentic workflows and multi-step tool use
Advanced software engineering and codebase-scale coding
Long-context analysis of large documents and repositories
Complex multimodal reasoning over text, images, audio, and video

pricing.note: Standard paid tier for prompts <=200k tokens; $4.00 input / $18.00 output for prompts >200k tokens.

faq.a: Gemini 3.1 Pro is Google DeepMind's flagship model, released February 19, 2026 as part of the Gemini 3 family. It targets the most complex reasoning, agentic, and coding tasks, accepts multimodal input across text, image, audio, video, and PDF, and offers a 1M-token input context window with up to 64K output tokens.

faq.q: How much does the Gemini 3.1 Pro API cost?
faq.a: On the standard paid tier, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview costs $2.00 per 1M input tokens and $12.00 per 1M output tokens for prompts up to 200k tokens. For prompts over 200k tokens, pricing rises to $4.00 input and $18.00 output per 1M tokens. Output pricing includes thinking tokens.

faq.q: What is the Gemini 3.1 Pro context window?
faq.a: Gemini 3.1 Pro supports an input context window of up to 1 million tokens and can produce up to 64K output tokens. The large context enables long-document analysis and reasoning over entire code repositories. Its training knowledge cutoff is January 2025.

No. Gemini 3.1 Pro is a proprietary, closed-weight model. It is available only through Google's services, including the Gemini API on Google AI Studio, Gemini CLI, Vertex AI, Gemini Enterprise, and the Gemini app for Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers. There are no downloadable weights.
